preFix.title: Fitting concealed kitchen cabinet hinges
preFix.subtitle: Drill the hinge cup, fit the clip-on plate, and adjust the hinge for a flush, level door
preFix.excerpt: How to fit and adjust concealed cup hinges on a kitchen cabinet door. Covers the hinge cup drill, fixing the mounting plate to the cabinet frame, and using the three adjustment screws to set the door level and flush.

content.text: is adjustable in three axes, the most forgiving cabinet hinge there is. Getting the cup hole position right matters; the three-way adjustment handles most small errors after fitting.
Step 1, Mark the cup hole positions

Mark the hinge positions on the door, typically 100 to 125 mm from the top and bottom edge of the door (more hinges are needed for tall or heavy doors). The cup centre should be 22 mm from the door edge, this gives the standard 4 mm overlay when the door sits on the face frame. Mark the centre point carefully.

in the cordless drill. Drill to a depth of 12 to 13 mm, the cup sits in the hole with 2 to 3 mm of floor remaining. Do not drill through. Hold the drill perpendicular to the door face throughout. If possible, use a depth stop on the drill or mark the bit with masking tape at 13 mm. Clean the hole of swarf before fitting the hinge.

Press the hinge cup into the drilled hole. The cup should sit flush or just below the door face. Mark and drill the two pilot holes for the cup fixing screws (typically 3.5 mm diameter). Drive the screws firmly. Do not overtighten into MDF or chipboard doors, the screws strip easily in thin board.

Mark the mounting plate positions on the inside face of the cabinet carcass at the same height as the cups on the door. The standard mounting plate position sets the door at 4 mm overlay on the frame. Drill pilot holes and screw the plates to the carcass.
Step 5, Hang and adjust the door

Clip the hinge onto the mounting plates and close the door. Check the gaps around the door: equal top and bottom, equal left and right. Use the three adjustment screws to correct any deviation, side adjustment moves the door along the frame face; depth adjustment moves it in or out; height adjustment moves it up or down. Work on one hinge at a time, holding the door shut between adjustments.
Inset door: the cup position moves to 10 to 12 mm from the edge rather than 22 mm, and a full-inset mounting plate sets the door flush inside the cabinet frame.
Corner cabinet: soft-close 165° hinges allow the door to open flat. The cup position is the same; the hinge arm is longer to swing the door fully clear of the adjacent cabinet.

items.fix: Deepen the cup hole 1 to 2 mm, or use the depth adjustment screw to move the door face outward.
items.cause: Cup hole too shallow or depth adjustment screw not set correctly
items.symptom: Door rubs on the cabinet frame when closing

items.fix: Use longer screws, or apply a small amount of PVA glue to the stripped holes and leave to cure before re-driving the screws.
items.symptom: Hinge cup screws strip in the door

initialFindings.message: PATTERN tutorial has no Method/Steps section and no orderedList — sequential instructions missing
